response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a capable generalist software engineer with a broad polyglot background and solid fundamentals, but the resume shows no meaningful alignment with the core domain of this role. There is no evidence of experimentation platform design, A/B testing infrastructure, feature flagging systems, telemetry pipelines, or statistical/metrics analysis — all of which are central to the Principal Growth/Experimentation Systems position. His high-scale systems work at Dunnhumby (high-throughput micro-services) is the closest relevant signal, but the scope and domain remain distant from growth engineering. The seniority claim is also weakened by the absence of cross-team technical leadership artifacts, measurable business outcomes, or documented influence at organizational scale.
strengths: Demonstrated high-performance, high-throughput systems experience at Dunnhumby handling millions of HTTP requests per second with low-latency requirements
Broad polyglot engineering background spanning C/C++, Java, TypeScript, D, Swift, and more — satisfies the language breadth requirement on paper
Open-source platform ownership (GISCollective, Fluent-Asserts, Trial) shows ability to design and ship developer-facing infrastructure independently
concerns: Zero experimentation platform experience: no A/B testing, feature flagging, rollout automation, bias detection, or experiment quality work appears anywhere in the resume
No telemetry or metrics infrastructure background: the role requires deep data aptitude and instrumentation system design, which are entirely absent from this profile
No evidence of IC6-level cross-team technical leadership, stakeholder alignment, or driving multi-workstream initiatives across product and data science organizations

key_factors.factor: No telemetry or metrics platform work
